message:
test(doom/mod): add 19 edge-case tests for DoomEngine lifecycle and Bresenham (bd-1yepr)

Cover Bresenham reverse directions, steep/gentle slopes, lifecycle edge
cases (zero dt, no-tick, render-without-update, flash full decay, all
controls), overlay branches (minimap without map, crosshair pixel
verification, zero-intensity flash skip), framebuffer resize on render,
various strides, and test map structure details.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1xo","title":"Windows Terminal compatibility layer","description":"PHASE 7 - POLISH: WINDOWS SUPPORT

## Background
Windows Terminal v1 support is deferred but must have clear integration path. Modern Windows Terminal supports ANSI, older conhost needs different handling.

## Technical Requirements (v1 Scope - Windows Terminal Only)
- Detect Windows Terminal vs legacy conhost
- Enable VT processing mode via SetConsoleMode
- Handle Windows-specific escape sequences
- PTY spawning via ConPTY API
- Signal handling differences (Ctrl+C, Ctrl+Break)
- Path handling for Windows conventions

## Out of Scope for v1
- Legacy conhost support (Windows 7/8)
- PowerShell-specific handling
- Windows-specific widgets

## Design Rationale
Per ADR-004, Windows v1 targets modern Windows Terminal only. This dramatically simplifies implementation while covering majority of Windows developer use.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Windows Terminal detection
- [ ] VT mode enabling
- [ ] ConPTY integration for PTY tests
- [ ] Signal handling shim
- [ ] CI testing on Windows
- [ ] Feature gate: windows

## Testing
- GitHub Actions Windows runner
- Manual testing on Windows Terminal
- Document known limitations

 {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.12","title":"perf(PlasmaFx): apply sin/cos decomposition to cell-space render_with_palette","description":"Goal
Apply the canvas adapter's proven sin/cos precomputation approach to the PlasmaFx cell-space render path (render_with_palette), eliminating per-pixel sin/cos calls.

Current State
PlasmaFx::render_with_palette (plasma.rs:646-708) has 3-4 sin/cos calls per pixel in the inner loop:
- v3: sin((x + y) * 1.2 + time_0_6) — 1 sin/pixel
- v6: sin(sin_x2[dx] * cos_y2 + time_0_5) — 1 sin/pixel (Reduced+Full)
- v4: sin(radial_center_phase[idx] - time_1_2) — 1 sin/pixel (Full only)
- v5: cos(radial_offset_phase[idx] + time) — 1 cos/pixel (Full only)

PlasmaCanvasAdapter (canvas_adapters.rs) already eliminated all inner-loop trig using sin(a+b) decomposition with precomputed sin/cos bases — zero trig calls per pixel.

Approach
1. Extend PlasmaScratch with sin/cos base arrays for each wave component
2. Precompute sin/cos bases in ensure_geometry (geometry-only, once per resize)
3. In render_with_palette, compute per-frame time sin/cos outside the loop
4. Replace inner-loop sin/cos with multiply-add using precomputed bases
5. Hoist quality branching outside the inner loop (match on quality, then loop)

Constraints
- PlasmaFx output will change at floating-point rounding level (acceptable — no golden hashes for this path)
- Must pass all existing PlasmaFx tests
- Must pass cargo check/clippy/fmt/test
- One lever: sin/cos decomposition only, no other changes

Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/plasma.rs (PlasmaScratch + render_with_palette)

Validation
- cargo bench -p ftui-extras --bench plasma_bench --features visual-fx (measure improvement)
- cargo test -p ftui-extras --features visual-fx plasma (pass all tests)
- cargo check/clippy/fmt","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureDesert","created_at":"2026-02-09T13:26:43.183834156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:39:16.302282861Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:39:16.302262913Z","close_reason":"Applied sin/cos decomposition to PlasmaFx cell-space render_with_palette. All trig precomputed at geometry time; inner loops use multiply-add only. 120x40 Full: 97µs (5.2x under 500µs budget). Throughput ~49 Melem/s.  {"id":"bd-3e1t.5.2","title":"Render-thread profiling + in-app timing for VFX","description":"Background
- We need precise, low-noise timing data for Visual Effects to drive real optimization.
- Flamegraphs alone don’t connect hotspots to frame phases; we need structured, in-app timing.

Goals
- Measure per-frame time spent in update, render, diff, and present for VFX runs.
- Keep instrumentation deterministic and behind a flag so normal demos remain clean.
- Produce JSONL logs that correlate with the VFX harness and golden hashes.

Scope / Work Items
- Add a perf timing toggle (env/CLI) used by the VFX harness.
- Instrument the VFX update/render pipeline with lightweight timers (no allocations in hot path).
- Emit per-frame JSONL events with: run_id, effect, size, tick_ms, frame_idx, update_ms, render_ms, diff_ms, present_ms, total_ms.
- Emit a summary event at end of run: count, p50/p95/p99 for total_ms and top phases.
- Ensure timers are monotonic and stable (avoid wall clock; prefer std::time::Instant).

Constraints
- No visual output changes, no quality/resolution reductions.
- When perf toggle is OFF, no extra logging or measurable overhead on hot path.
- Deterministic: log values must match frame order and inputs.

Tests / Validation
- Unit test for the summary aggregator (p50/p95/p99 on a fixed dataset).
- E2E test: run harness with perf enabled and validate JSONL schema + required fields.
- Ensure missing fields or non-monotonic frame_idx fails fast with clear errors.

Notes
- This bead is a prerequisite for accurate hotspot selection in bd-3e1t.5.3.","acceptance_criteria":"1) With perf flag enabled, JSONL logs include per-frame timing fields and a summary record with p50/p95/p99.
2) With perf flag disabled, timing logs are absent and demo output is unchanged.
3) The VFX harness can consume and validate these logs in an automated E2E run.
4) Summary aggregator is unit-tested and produces deterministic results.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:07.537077007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T23:10:20.288638162Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T23:10:20.288617663Z","close_reason":"Implemented VFX perf timing JSONL + summary + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:07.537077007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:13.104642394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-3e1t.5.3","title":"VFX hotspot matrix + algorithmic optimization pass","description":"Background
- We need dramatic VFX performance gains without any visual changes.
- Optimization must be data-driven: measure → pick hotspot → change one lever → prove identical output.

Goals
- Use profiling data to target the top VFX hotspots and reduce frame time substantially.
- Maintain strict isomorphism: identical inputs produce identical outputs (hashes must match).

Scope / Work Items
- Build a hotspot/opportunity matrix using perf logs + flamegraph:
  - List top 5 hotspots (function + line).
  - For each: Impact (1-5), Confidence (1-5), Effort (1-5), Score = (Impact×Confidence)/Effort.
- Select only Score ≥ 2.0 items for implementation.
- Apply a single, isolated optimization per change (cache, reuse, algorithmic improvement, etc.).
- After each change: reprofile and verify hashes with the golden registry.
- Document an isomorphism proof per change (ordering, tie-breakers, RNG/time).

Constraints
- No quality/resolution degradation, no visual output changes.
- Deterministic output required; hashes must be unchanged unless explicitly blessed.
- Avoid adding new files unless absolutely necessary; prefer existing logs/tests.

Tests / Validation
- Use harness + golden hashes (bd-3e1t.5.1 + bd-3e1t.5.5).
- E2E run must report p50/p95/p99 improvement and matching hashes.
- If hashes differ, provide a deterministic root-cause analysis before any bless.

Success Target (Performance)
- Achieve a clearly measurable improvement: ≥30% reduction in p95 total frame time for at least two heavy effects (e.g., plasma/metaballs/doom/quake) under the harness.

Notes
- Always reprofile after each optimization; hotspots will shift.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Hotspot/opportunity matrix captured for top 5 VFX hotspots with scores.
2) At least one Score ≥ 2.0 optimization implemented with a single-lever change.
3) Harness shows ≥30% p95 total frame time reduction for at least two heavy effects.
4) Golden hashes remain identical (or are explicitly blessed with documented rationale).
5) Isomorphism proof recorded for each change.","notes":"BrightDeer taking an additional optimization pass: single-lever change, deterministic behavior, full gates.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightDeer","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:57:22.055503264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:16.221343258Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:05:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:05:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:05:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":992,"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.5.3","author":"HazyBrook","text":"Starting work: focusing on Metaballs/Plasma hot loops in crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/effects/canvas_adapters.rs. **Doom renderer:**
- Removed redundant fb.clear() before draw_background() (saves width×height pixel writes/frame)
- Hoisted loop-invariant computations from per-column loop: ceil_h, floor_h, pitch_offset, sx_span, vx_span, back_ceil, back_floor, has_upper, has_lower
- Added per-row background gradient cache (eliminates 6× lerp_u8 per row per frame after first render)

**Quake renderer:**
- Replaced fb.clear() with fb.clear_depth() (saves width×height pixel writes, keeps z-buffer reset)
- Hoisted per-triangle edge deltas (e12_dy/dx, e20_dy/dx) and per-row fy-dependent terms from rasterizer inner loop
- New QuakeFramebuffer::clear_depth() method for targeted z-buffer reset

**Also fixed pre-existing clippy issues:** wad.rs unnecessary_cast, framebuffer.rs identity_op

All changes preserve exact FP output (determinism/hash parity maintained). Quality gates: cargo check + clippy + fmt + 620 tests passing.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:39:01Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3e1t.5.3","title":"VFX hotspot matrix + algorithmic optimization pass","description":"Background
- We need dramatic VFX performance gains without any visual changes.
- Optimization must be data-driven: measure → pick hotspot → change one lever → prove identical output.

Goals
- Use profiling data to target the top VFX hotspots and reduce frame time substantially.
- Maintain strict isomorphism: identical inputs produce identical outputs (hashes must match).

Scope / Work Items
- Build a hotspot/opportunity matrix using perf logs + flamegraph:
  - List top 5 hotspots (function + line).
  - For each: Impact (1-5), Confidence (1-5), Effort (1-5), Score = (Impact×Confidence)/Effort.
- Select only Score ≥ 2.0 items for implementation.
- Apply a single, isolated optimization per change (cache, reuse, algorithmic improvement, etc.).
- After each change: reprofile and verify hashes with the golden registry.
- Document an isomorphism proof per change (ordering, tie-breakers, RNG/time).

Constraints
- No quality/resolution degradation, no visual output changes.
- Deterministic output required; hashes must be unchanged unless explicitly blessed.
- Avoid adding new files unless absolutely necessary; prefer existing logs/tests.

Tests / Validation
- Use harness + golden hashes (bd-3e1t.5.1 + bd-3e1t.5.5).
- E2E run must report p50/p95/p99 improvement and matching hashes.
- If hashes differ, provide a deterministic root-cause analysis before any bless.

Success Target (Performance)
- Achieve a clearly measurable improvement: ≥30% reduction in p95 total frame time for at least two heavy effects (e.g., plasma/metaballs/doom/quake) under the harness.

Notes
- Always reprofile after each optimization; hotspots will shift.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Hotspot/opportunity matrix captured for top 5 VFX hotspots with scores.
2) At least one Score ≥ 2.0 optimization implemented with a single-lever change.

4) Golden hashes remain identical (or are explicitly blessed with documented rationale).

Scope: add span storage to Buffer, merge policy, overflow fallback to full-row scan, and deterministic scan order identical to current row-major changes list.  {"id":"bd-3e1t.6.1","title":"Spec: dirty-span model, invariants, and merge policy","description":"Background: dirty-row tracking is too coarse for sparse updates. Task: specify per-row span data model, merge/union policy, max span cap, overflow fallback to full-row scan, deterministic ordering, and reset semantics via clear_dirty. Considerations: scissor/opacity interactions, optional guard bands, config defaults, and evidence log fields. Output: written spec captured in notes.","notes":"Dirty-Span Model (Spec v1)

Motivation
- Dirty-row tracking is too coarse for sparse updates: scanning full rows wastes work.
- Dirty-span tracking records per-row modified x-ranges to reduce diff scan cost while preserving soundness.

Data Model
- For each row y, maintain a small ordered list of spans: Span { x0: u16, x1: u16 } half-open.
- Spans are non-overlapping, sorted by x0 ascending.
- Per-row full_row/overflow flag indicates the row should be scanned fully; when true, spans list is empty.
- Optional guard band (merge_gap) merges spans that are within ≤ merge_gap cells.

Invariants (Must Hold)
1) Soundness: for any cell (x,y) mutated since last clear, ∃ span in row y such that x ∈ [x0,x1).
2) Sorted + non-overlapping spans per row (after merge).
3) Deterministic merge order: given the same mutation sequence, span lists are identical.
4) Full-row flag dominates: if full_row[y] = true, spans[y] is empty and row is scanned fully.

Merge / Union Policy
- Insert span (x0,x1) into row y:
  - If full_row[y] => no-op (already full).
  - Insert at position by x0 (binary search recommended).
  - Merge with neighbors when overlaps or when gap ≤ merge_gap.
  - After merge, if span_count > max_spans_per_row => set full_row[y]=true and clear spans.
- Default implementation values (current): merge_gap = 1, max_spans_per_row = 64.
- Optional future: make these configurable; add coverage threshold to flip to full_row.

Mutation API (Conceptual)
- mark_dirty_span(y, x0, x1)
- mark_dirty_cell(y, x) => mark_dirty_span(y, x, x+1)
- mark_dirty_rect(rect): for each row in rect, call mark_dirty_span(y, rect.x, rect.right())

Reset Semantics
- clear_dirty_spans(): clears all spans and full_row flags.
- On Buffer::new, initialize all rows as full_row to guarantee first diff is full.

Interaction with Existing Dirty Rows
- Dirty-span is a strict refinement; dirty_rows is a coarse fallback.
- If both exist, diff strategy may choose:
  - full_row[y] => full scan (row-skip still allowed)
  - spans[y].is_empty() => skip row
  - spans[y] => scan only spans
- False positives are allowed; false negatives are forbidden.

Scissor / Opacity / Compositing
- Only record spans for actual writes that pass scissor checks.
- Opacity compositing may produce no net change, but still mark span; false positives are OK.
- When scissor stacks are used, mark spans only for the intersected row segments.

Determinism & Ordering
- Use Vec/SmallVec per row to avoid hash-based ordering.
- Merge order is deterministic: left-to-right; stable tie-breakers for equal x0.

Evidence / Telemetry Fields (proposed)
- dirty_span_rows, dirty_span_total, dirty_span_overflows
- max_spans_per_row, merge_gap, span_coverage_cells
- rows_full_dirty

Failure Modes + Fallbacks
- Excessive fragmentation => full_row fallback
- Span list overflow => full_row fallback
- Resize => re-init to full dirty

Tests (Spec-level)
- Unit: merge behavior (overlap, adjacency, guard band, overflow)
- Property: soundness vs random mutation sets (spans cover all mutated cells)
- Regression: deterministic output for identical mutation sequences
- E2E: span stats logged in JSONL under fixed seed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:46.856751717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:30:27.225108113Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:16:10.481846217Z","close_reason":"Completed dirty-span spec in docs/spec/state-machines.md section 3.14","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e1t.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3e1t.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:52:46.856751717Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
